summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that a crafted .mcpb file with path traversal in manifest.name causes the extracted content to be relocated outside the intended upload directory via fs.renameSync.
expected_security_impact: Uploading a .mcpb file with manifest.name containing ../ sequences should cause the extracted files to be moved to a directory outside data/uploads/mcpb/, demonstrating arbitrary filesystem write via path traversal.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh builds and launches MCPHub, authenticates, uploads a crafted .mcpb file with manifest.name set to ../../../../../tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal, and the extracted content is relocated to /tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal outside the intended upload directory.
verification_method: The exploit script checks for the existence of the directory at the traversal target path (/tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal) and verifies it contains the expected proof string EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_PROOF in the extracted files. The verification distinguishes actual path traversal from normal extraction by checking whether the final directory is outside the upload base directory.
evidence_produced_by_run: A directory was created at the traversal target path outside the intended upload directory
The extracted files contain the proof string EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_PROOF
The upload response confirmed successful processing of the malicious .mcpb file
limitations_or_blockers: The exploit requires git clone access to the target repository for the run.sh host-side script
The exploit requires Docker to be available on the host

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
